items.title: 'A footballer's dream': From humble beginings to the world stage

children.content: The Touré brothers snuck into their local football field after hours to play the game they loved, while the Yengi brothers kicked around makeshift balls made of plastic bags. Two of them have now made it onto the world stage as Socceroos.

gallery.altText: composite of a Mo Toure as a young boy in a soccer field and him as an adult in a Socceroos guernsey
Young brothers play and laugh together in their family driveway
Tete Yengi celebrates scoring a goal

thumbnail.altText: composite of a Mo Toure as a young boy in a soccer field and him as an adult in a Socceroos guernsey
Young brothers play and laugh together in their family driveway
Tete Yengi celebrates scoring a goal

items.title: SANFL players consider industrial action as hourly salary drops to $8

children.content: The SANFL Players' Association is discussing the potential of a strike in response to a 50 per cent decline in pay over the past 20 years.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit and tie sitting on a bench in a grassed area

items.title: Reuben Kaye reckons you can joke about anything — 'it's just how you do it'

children.content: The new artistic director of the Adelaide Cabaret Festival shares what's unique about the art form in Australia and what his teenage self would think about who he is today.

image.altText: A portrait of Reuben Kaye, 41, smiling, mouth open, one finger between his teeth, with a cheeky expression.

items.title: Gus Lamont's grandparent fined over gun offence, unrelated to 4yo's disappearance

children.content: The grandparent of missing four-year-old Gus Lamont has been fined $10,500 for possession of a gun silencer at the family's Oak Park Station, in South Australia's far north. Police have previously said the offence is not related to his disappearance.

image.altText: Josie Murray bumping into reporters outside Adelaide Magistrates Court.

gallery.altText: A person wearing with shoulder-length blonde hair wearing an Akubra hat and maroon jacket, holding a black handbag

thumbnail.altText: A person wearing with shoulder-length blonde hair wearing an Akubra hat and maroon jacket, holding a black handbag

avatar.alt: A woman with blonde hair wearing a black blazer and white top

items.title: Feral sambar deer likely brought to SA for hunting purposes

children.content: One of Australia's largest feral deer species was spotted in SA's Riverland for the first time earlier this month. While its existence is being investigated, the Invasive Species Council says it was likely transported across state lines for hunting purposes.

image.altText: Fallow deer seen through a rifle scope.

items.title: Ex pro cyclist Rohan Dennis reported for driving while disqualified, ABC understands

children.content: Rohan Dennis was handed a suspended sentence last year for the events leading up to the death of his Olympian wife Melissa Hoskins in December 2023.

image.altText: A white sedan on the tray of a tow truck parked in front of a police car

items.title: Port River 'stud muffin' among spate of dolphin deaths in Adelaide

children.content: Environmental authorities are investigating what caused the death of a Port River dolphin, believed to be Zoom, this week after several dolphin carcasses washed up on Adelaide beaches in recent days.

image.altText: The fin of a dolphin coming up to surface in a murky river with green mangroves on the shore.

items.title: Parts of Australia on track for warmest start to winter on record

children.content: An influx of humid, tropical air has resulted in a remarkably warm and relatively wet start to winter across Australia.

image.altText: Blue skies over the ocean at Port Willunga in South Australia

items.title: NDIS labour hire company enters administration, 480 jobs in limbo

children.content: NDIS labour hire provider United Foundation has entered voluntary administration blaming regulatory changes, delayed payments and rising costs.

image.altText: A man in a wheelchair holding a cane next to someone walking and wearing jeans.

items.title: 'Silly thing': Man fined thousands for dumping tyres in heritage-listed area

children.content: A young man who could have paid $115 to discard a trailer load of tyres has been fined thousands for illegally dumping them down a mine shaft in a national heritage-listed area — and ordered to clean it up.

image.altText: A pile of old tyres sitting on an empty lot.

items.title: Bushies reunite for bikes, beers and banter as record rain lifts spirits

children.content: The trip to Marree in South Australia looked a little different for competitors in this year's annual gymkhana and motorkhana, with the outback blooming after record-breaking rain.

gallery.altText: A man wearing a cowboy hat smiling to the right of screen, leaning against a fence
A man on a dirt track doing a wheelie on a motorbike
A sillouhette of a motorbike wheel coming off a platform with dust around

thumbnail.altText: A man wearing a cowboy hat smiling to the right of screen, leaning against a fence
A man on a dirt track doing a wheelie on a motorbike
A sillouhette of a motorbike wheel coming off a platform with dust around

items.title: Line dancing resurgence draws gen Z out to socialise in Adelaide

children.content: It's seen by many as a way to socialise and meet new people in an era where such activities are at a low point due to the ongoing impacts of COVID-19, screen time and social media.

image.altText: A large group of people dance on a dance floor at a pub

items.title: Fraudsters jailed for stealing millions from 'older and vulnerable' victims

children.content: Two men who "exploited the vulnerability" of multiple victims by swindling millions of dollars in a fraudulent property investment scheme have been jailed for the "undoubtedly serious" offending.

image.altText: A composite image of a bald man and a man with a beard

items.title: Consumer watchdog warns real estate agents must not misrepresent homes

children.content: Real estate agents selling homes must not misrepresent information about the builder or serious defects, SA's consumer watchdog has said, after eight builders were hit with public warning notices over their conduct in the state.

gallery.altText: A skirting board with a gap to a door frame

thumbnail.altText: A skirting board with a gap to a door frame

items.title: Adelaide defence lawyer pleads not guilty to sexual offences

children.content: Andrew Graham enters not guilty pleas to two counts of rape and one charge of compelling a person to sexually manipulate an offender.

image.altText: Andrew Graham is speaking outside a sandstone coloured court building

items.title: SA locks in AFL Gather Round until 2029 but no decision on venues

children.content: The AFL's Gather Round will stay in South Australia until 2029 under a new three-year partnership. It will include a parade and a "feature match" but no decision has been made on venues.

image.altText: Children wearing AFL jumpers run through a large blue banner saying GATHER ROUND TO STAY IN SA

items.title: Bakery trains staff to assess counterfeit notes amid rise in use in SA

children.content: An Adelaide business owner who has been targeted by counterfeiters about a dozen times is warning others to be vigilant, as SA Police warn the number of incidents has increased in the state in the past year.

image.altText: A close-up of a hand holding a $50 note and shining an blue UV light on the clear strip.

items.title: Predictive text in 'demonstrable decline' with AI-based language models

children.content: If internet chatter is to be believed, the intuitiveness and efficiency of predictive text and autocorrect have fallen off a cliff in recent years. But while researchers agree there has been a demonstrable decline in quality, the increasing sophistication of what phones can do is also making errors more prominent.

image.altText: a close up of a person holding a mobile phone and texting

items.title: Brother and sister from remote SA town receive King's Birthday Honours

children.content: A brother and sister who grew up near the rural town of Lock on the Eyre Peninsula both received honours over the King's Birthday long weekend.

image.altText: A woman stands next to a sign in a rural area. A paddock is behind her.

items.title: How Israel's operation 'Eternal Darkness' in southern Lebanon is expanding

children.content: The so-called ceasefire since April, which Hezbollah is not a party to, has done little to halt the death and destruction in southern Lebanon. Here's how the scale of the Israeli operation called "Eternal Darkness" continues to expand.

image.altText: A green landscape dotted with houses in southern Lebanon is seen from Metula.

items.title: Grandfather of child rescued from Syria pleads for 'stability, love, care'

children.content: Jasmine was five years old and so traumatised that when she woke up trying to scream, she couldn't make a sound.

gallery.altText: Illustration shows a child holding a doll and the hand of an adult in a refugee camp.

thumbnail.altText: Illustration shows a child holding a doll and the hand of an adult in a refugee camp.

avatar.alt: Reporter James Oaten wearing a navy button-up shirt.

items.title: A man fled a 'bounty' in Canberra. Months later, he was dead in Brisbane

children.content: Neil Tracey's death was ruled an overdose in what investigators would call an open and shut case. His family are questioning if police knew about the bounty that led him to flee his hometown.

image.altText: Graphic of a man's smiling face with newspapers articles surrounding it.

items.title: Parts of Australia on track for warmest start to winter on record

children.content: An influx of humid, tropical air has resulted in a remarkably warm and relatively wet start to winter across Australia.

image.altText: Blue skies over the ocean at Port Willunga in South Australia

items.title: Villages 700 years old destroyed to 'uproot people' from their history

children.content: Non-governmental organisations Biladi and Blue Shield are among the design experts using new technologies to create detailed records of cultural heritage and help rebuild after conflict.

gallery.altText: An older Middle Eastern man, despondent, stands amid the debris of a destroyed church in Lebanon.

thumbnail.altText: An older Middle Eastern man, despondent, stands amid the debris of a destroyed church in Lebanon.

avatar.alt: Hannah Story, a blonde woman sitting outside smiles. A dog is at her feet, and she wears a T-shirt that reads "MIRANDA JULY".

items.title: What Australia would have looked like when the dinosaurs roamed

children.content: Palaeontologists say evidence pointing to Australia's polar past shows that many dinosaurs roamed the country when the south east was still within the Antarctic Circle.

image.altText: Artist's impression of a large carnivorous dinosaur hunting two smaller dinosaurs.

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Testing your knowledge of ABBA, the World Cup mascots and some fruity language

children.content: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

items.title: The big life lessons you've learnt since owning a pet

children.content: Pets can be lifelong companions, but they can also test us in ways we never imagined. We asked you to share your stories. Here's what you told us.

image.altText: A big, friendly looking Australian shepherd dog sits on an outdoor couch, being hugged by smiling woman.

items.title: Australian sprinter's death in Thailand came weeks after her 25th birthday

children.content: The brother of Stawell Gift finalist Jemma Stapleton remembers her "bright smile" and expresses gratitude for the outpouring of grief and support.

image.altText: An athlete holds her hands up next to head and smiles.

items.title: Coastal town hires its own film crew to capture life in 2026

children.content: Schoolchildren starring in a documentary about their quaint home town on the Queensland coast hope the publicity does not change what they love about it.

A woman smiles with instruments behind her
